name: Whole Winter Melon Soup B1
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the winter melon and slice off the lid 3 inches from the stem.
2. Scoop out the seeds and pulp in the center, then make tiger-teeth notches 3/4 inch deep around the rim of the melon.
3. Pare off the outer skin from the teeth, cut the fleshy part into small pieces, and use it in the soup.
4. Place the melon in an ovenproof bowl and set aside.
5. In a deep kettle large enough to hold the melon, place the broth, 2 cans water, chicken breast, lotus seeds, ham, and bamboo shoots into the melon.
6. Pour 3 inches of boiling water into the bottom of the kettle.
7. Place a kitchen towel in the bottom of the kettle and set the bowl on it, lifting up all four corners over the melon.
8. When done cooking, lift out the bowl with the melon by the corners of the towel.
9. Cover and steam the melon over medium heat for 2 hours.
10. After the first hour, check the water level; if not at 3 inches, add boiling water.
11. When the melon is done (the white meat on its lining will be translucent), add the abalone and steam for an additional minute before serving.
12. To serve, scoop the flesh carefully from the sides of the melon and ladle with broth into soup bowls.
Temperature: HOT
Effort: AVERAGE
Time: 02:30
